verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “The people answered Him, We have heard out of the Law that Christ abideth for ever; and how sayest thou, The Son of Man must be lifted up?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Who is this Son of Man?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Deceit is a thing easily detected, and weak, though it be daubed outside with ten thousand colors.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For as those who whitewash decayed walls, cannot by the plastering make them sound, so too those who lie are easily found out, as in fact was the case here with the Jews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when Christ said to them, “If I be lifted up I will draw all men unto Me; We have heard,” saith one of them, “out of the Law, that Christ remaineth forever; and how sayest thou, that the Son of Man must be lifted up?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Who is this Son of Man?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Even they then knew that Christ was some Immortal One, and had life without end.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And therefore they also knew what He meant; for often in Scripture the Passion and the Resurrection are mentioned in the same place.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thus Isaiah puts them together, saying, “He was led as a sheep to the slaughter” ( Isa. liii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 7 ), and all that follows.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: David also in the second Psalm, and in many other places, connects these two things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The Patriarch too after saying, “He lay down, He couched as a lion,” addeth, “And as a lion’s whelp, who shall raise Him up?” ( Gen. xlix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He showeth at once the Passion and the Resurrection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But these men when they thought to silence Him, and to show that He was not the Christ, confessed by this very circumstance that the Christ remaineth forever.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And observe their evil dealing; they said not, “We have heard that Christ neither suffereth nor is crucified,” but that “He remaineth forever.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet even this which has been mentioned, would have been no real objection, for the Passion was no hindrance to His Immortality.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hence we may see that they understood many of the doubtful points, and deliberately went wrong.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since He had before spoken about death, when they now heard in this place the, “be lifted up,” they guessed that death was referred to.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then they said, “Who is this Son of Man?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This too they did deceitfully.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Think not, I pray,” saith one, “that we say this concerning thee, assert not that we oppose thee through enmity, for, lo, we know not concerning whom thou speakest, and still we declare our opinion.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then doth Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To silence them, and to show that the Passion is no impediment to His enduring forever, He saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Yet a little while,” He saith, “is the light with you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Signifying that His death was a removal; for the light of the sun is not destroyed, but having retired for a while appears again.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Walk while ye have the light.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Of what season doth He here speak?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Of the whole present life, or of the time before the Crucifixion?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I for my part think of both, for on account of His unspeakable lovingkindness, many even after the Crucifixion believed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And He speaketh these things to urge them on to the faith, as He also did before, saying, “Yet a little while I am with you.” ( c. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He that walketh in darkness knoweth not whither he goeth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How many things, for instance, even now do the Jews, without knowing what they do, but walking as though they were in darkness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They think that they are going the right way, when they are taking the contrary; keeping the Sabbath, respecting the Law and the observances about meats, yet knowing not whither they walk.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore He said,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Walk in the light, that ye may become children of the light.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That is, “My children.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet in the beginning the Evangelist saith, “Were born, not of bloods, nor of the will of the flesh, but of God” ( c. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 13 ); that is, of the Father; while here Himself is said to beget them; that thou mayest understand that the operation of the Father and the Son is One.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Jesus having spoken these things,” departed from them, and did hide Himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why doth He now “hide Himself”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: They took not up stones against Him, nor did they blaspheme Him in any such manner as before; why then did He hide Himself?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Walking in men’s hearts, He knew that their wrath was fierce, though they said nothing; He knew it boiling and murderous, and waited not till it issued into action, but hid Himself, to allay their ill-will.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe how the Evangelist has alluded to this feeling; he has immediately added,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Though He had done so many miracles, they believed not on Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What “so many”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So many as the Evangelist hath omitted.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this is clear also from what follows.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when He had retired, and given in, and had come to them again, He speaketh with them in a lowly manner, saying, “He that believeth on Me, believeth not on Me, but on Him that sent Me.” ( Ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe what He doeth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He beginneth with humble and modest expressions, and betaketh Himself to the Father; then again He raiseth His language, and when He seeth that they are exasperated, He retireth; then He cometh to them again, and again beginneth with words of humility.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And where hath He done this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nay, where hath He not done it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: See, for instance, what He saith at the beginning, “As I hear, I judge.” ( c. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then in a loftier tone, “As the Father raiseth up the dead, and quickeneth them, so also the Son quickeneth whom He will” ( c. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 21 ); again, “I judge you not, there is another that judgeth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then again He retireth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then coming to Galilee, “Labor not,” He saith, “for the meat that perisheth” ( c. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 27 ); and after having said great things of Himself, that He came down from Heaven, that He giveth eternal life, He again withdraweth Himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And He cometh in the Feast of Tabernacles also, and doth the same.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And one may see Him continually thus varying His teaching, by His presence, by His absence, by lowly, by high discourses.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Which He also did here.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Though He had done so many miracles,” it saith, “they believed not on Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “That the saying of Esaias might be fulfilled which he spake, Lord, who hath believed our report, and to whom hath the arm of the Lord been revealed?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “They could not believe,” it saith, “because that Esaias said, Ye shall hear with your ears, and not understand.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: These things he said, when he saw His glory, and spake of Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here again observe, that the “because,” and “spake,” refer not to the cause of their unbelief, but to the event.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it was not “because” Isaiah spake, that they believed not; but because they were not about to believe, that he spake.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why then doth not the Evangelist express it so, instead of making the unbelief proceed from the prophecy, not the prophecy from the unbelief?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And farther on he putteth this very thing more positively, saying, “Therefore they could not believe, because that Esaias said.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He desires hence to establish by many proofs the unerring truth of Scripture, and that what Isaiah foretold fell not out otherwise, but as he said.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For lest any one should say, “Wherefore did Christ come?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Knew he not that they would give no heed to him?” he introduces the Prophets, who knew this also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But He came that they might have no excuse for their sin; for what things the Prophet foretold, he foretold as certainly to be; since if they were not certainly to be, he could not have foretold them; and they were certainly to be, because these men were incurable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if, “they could not,” is put, instead of, “they would not,” do not marvel, for He saith also in another place, “He that is able to receive it, let him receive it.” ( Matt. xix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So in many places He is wont to term choice, power.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Again, “The world cannot hate you, but Me it hateth.” ( c. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This one may even see observed in common conversation; as when a man saith, “I cannot love this or that person,” calling the force of his will, power.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And again, “this or that person cannot be a good man.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And what saith the Prophet?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If the Ethiopian shall change his skin, or the leopard his spots, this people also shall be able to do good, having learned evil.” ( Jer. xiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He saith not that the doing of virtue is impossible to them, but that because they will not, therefore they cannot.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And by what he saith the Evangelist means, that it was impossible for the Prophet to lie; yet it was not on that account impossible that they should believe.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it was possible, even had they believed, that he should remain true; since he would not have prophesied these things if they had been about to believe.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Why then,” saith some one, “did he not say so?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because Scripture hath certain idiomatic phrases of this kind, and it is needful to make allowance for its laws.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “The seethings he spake when he saw His glory.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The Father’s.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then doth John speak of the Son? and Paul of the Spirit?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not as confounding the Persons, but as showing that the Dignity is one, they say it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For that which is the Father’s is the Son’s also, and that which is the Son’s is the Spirit’s.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet many things God spake by Angels, and no one saith, “as the Angel spake,” but how? “as God spake.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since what hath been said by God through the ministry of Angels would be of God; yet not therefore is what is of God, of the Angels also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But in this place John saith that the words are the Spirit’s.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And spake of Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What spake he?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I saw the Lord sitting upon a high throne” ( Isa. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 1 ), and what follows.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore he there calleth “glory,” that vision, the smoke, the hearing unutterable Mysteries, the beholding the Seraphim, the lightning which leaped from the throne, against which those powers could not look.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And spake of Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What said he?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That he heard a voice, saying, “Whom shall I send? who shall go?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And I said, Here am I, send me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And He said, Ye shall hear with your ears, and shall not understand, and seeing ye shall see, and not perceive.” ( Isa. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He hath blinded their eyes, and hardened their heart, lest they at any time should see with their eyes, and understand with their heart.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here again is another question, but it is not so if we rightly consider it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For as the sun dazzles the eyes of the weak, not by reason of its proper nature, so it is with those who give not heed to the words of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thus, in the case of Pharaoh, He is said to have hardened his heart, and so it is with those who are at all contentious against the words of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This is a peculiar mode of speech in Scripture, as also the, “He gave them over unto a reprobate mind” ( Rom. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 28 ), and the, “He divided them to the nations,” that is, allowed, permitted them to go.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the writer doth not here introduce God as Himself working these things, but showeth that they took place through the wickedness of others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For, when we are abandoned by God, we are given up to the devil, and when so given up, we suffer ten thousand dreadful things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To terrify the hearer, therefore, the writer saith, “He hardened,” and “gave over.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For to show that He doth not only not give us over, but doth not even leave us, except we will it, hear what He saith, “Do not your iniquities separate between Me and you?” ( Isa. lix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And again, “They that go far away from Thee shall perish.” ( Ps. lxxiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And Hosea saith, “Thou hast forgotten the law of thy God, and I will also forget thee” ( Hos. iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 6 , LXX.); and He saith Himself also in the Gospels, “How often would I have gathered your children—and ye would not.” ( Luke xiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Esaias also again, “I came, and there was no man; I called, and there was none to hearken.” ( Isa. l.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: These things He saith, showing that we begin the desertion, and become the causes of our perdition; for God not only desireth not to leave or to punish us, but even when He punisheth, doth it unwillingly; “I will not,” He saith, “the death of a sinner, so much as that he should turn and live.” ( Ezek. xviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Christ also mourneth over the destruction of Jerusalem, as we also do over our friends.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Knowing this, let us do all so as not to remove from God, but let us hold fast to the care of our souls, and to the love towards each other; let us not tear our own members, (for this is the act of men insane and beside themselves,) but the more we see any ill disposed, the more let us be kind to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since we often see many persons suffering in their bodies from difficult or incurable maladies, and cease not to apply remedies.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What is worse than gout in foot or hand?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Are we therefore to cut off the limbs?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not at all, but we use every means that the sufferer may enjoy some comfort, since we cannot get rid of the disease.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This also let us do in the case of our brethren, and, even though they be diseased incurably, let us continue to tend them, and let us bear one another’s burdens.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So shall we fulfill the law of Christ, and obtain the promised good things, through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, with whom to the Father and the Holy Ghost be glory for ever and ever.
